initial_diagnostic_question.question: Does this ECG suggest the presence of typical premature ventricular complex(es)?

criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose typical premature ventricular complexes, which of the following diagnostic criteria should be evaluated?
options: Presence of inverted T waves in inferior leads
Presence of intermittent ventricular pauses (dropped beats)
Presence of an irregularly irregular ventricular rhythm
Presence of a consistently regular RR interval

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, are there any premature beats on this ECG, where a QRS complex occurs earlier than expected time based on the preceding normal cycles?

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe the premature beat? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this ECG suggest the presence of typical premature ventricular complex(es)?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
decision.answer: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis

criterion_selection.question: In addition to the finding you just identified, which other diagnostic criterion should be evaluated to diagnose typical premature ventricular complexes?
options: Presence of a Delta wave (slurred upstroke) on the premature beat
Presence of ST elevation in the premature beat
Presence of inverted T waves in inferior leads
Presence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats
Absence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats
criterion_selection.answer: Presence of significant QRS prolongation in the premature beat compared to normal beats

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, for the QRS complex in the selected segment, is the QRS duration prolonged significantly compared to the normal beats on this ECG?

grounding.question: Which range does the measured QRS duration for the premature beat fall into?

decision.question: Based on all the findings identified so far, does this ECG suggest the presence of typical premature ventricular complex(es)?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
